item.title: Your chance to buy alleged MFS fraudster Paresh Raja’s Ferrari
item.description: Eight used supercars, one incautious owner

item.title: Brussels unveils sweeping plan to boost Europe’s digital sovereignty
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Counterfeit cigarette use surges across Europe, new report finds

item.title: The risk manager who revived Deutsche Bank
item.description: The European bank has finally learned that it doesn’t need the US to reach success

item.title: How can the UK catch up on stablecoins?
item.description: Inquiry warns country risks missing out on potential benefits of new form of digital money because of excessive regulation

item.title: Florentino Pérez: Real Madrid offers investors prestige not profits
item.description: Club president says illustrious brand will lure outside capital at €10bn-plus valuation as he fights for re-election

item.title: New York Knicks title run gives besieged owner a rare reprieve
item.description: Long assailed for banning fans who yell ‘sell the team!’, James Dolan gets a break after decades without a championship

item.title: Andrew Left’s fraud conviction leaves Wall Street asking: what are the rules?
item.description: Guilty verdict for famed short seller raises questions on line between public commentary and illegal trading

item.title: Can the rush for vaccines slow the latest Ebola outbreak?
item.description: The Bundibugyo strain is a reminder that serious health threats can escalate from under-the-radar pathogens

item.title: Swedish business bristles over EU interventions
item.description: Lawyers and financiers complain that policymakers could be undermining the success of the country’s model

item.title: Inside Alexandr Wang’s bid to revive Meta’s AI edge
item.description: Muse Spark model has brought momentum, but doubts linger over whether billionaire prodigy can close the gap with rivals

item.title: Kazakh billionaire under SEC scrutiny seeks to build European digital bank
item.description: Application from Freedom Holdings for French banking licence comes as US watchdog examines enforcement action

item.title: A guide for the perplexed on AI
item.description: Should we think of this technology as boon, bane or bubble?

item.title: The AI boom gives 1990s IT brands a second crack at youth
item.description: Top-line growth is exciting Wall Street even though the groups are just one link in a much larger AI-related domino rally

item.title: Index investing will evolve with mega IPOs
item.description: New offerings will be a test but markets ultimately absorb new supply and determine prices over time

item.title: Trading firms generated $114bn in revenue last year
item.description: Companies such as Jane Street and Citadel Securities continue to push into territory once dominated by banks

item.title: Will the Middle East crisis save Jim Ratcliffe’s Ineos empire?
item.description: Some bond investors believe the group’s debt is unsustainable, but others think its scale and edge will see it through

item.title: Can we trust AI to build a better version of itself?
item.description: Industry chiefs say ‘recursive self-improvement’ is approaching. Safety experts say we’re not ready

item.title: MFS administrators clash over Apollo-backed plan to reclaim lost billions
item.description: Restructuring firms fail to agree on strategy to claw back £1.3bn allegedly misappropriated by MFS founder Paresh Raja

item.title: What happens when heat pump installations go wrong
item.description: Botched work by some of the country’s officially approved contractors dent confidence in key part of government’s energy transition plan

item.title: Tim Hayward: I built the Jaguar E-Type of computer keyboards
item.description: The FT columnist enters the world of bespoke customisation

item.title: Why Richard Nixon torpedoed the global monetary system
item.description: All the gold in Fort Knox wasn’t enough to save the US dollar

item.title: AI Labs: Zuckerberg’s $100bn gamble
item.description: Can Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta buy its way into the AI game?

item.title: CBS fires Scott Pelley after clash over ‘60 Minutes’
item.description: Sacking escalates bitter confrontation between newsroom and editor-in-chief Bari Weiss

item.title: Palantir should not have ‘significant role’ in UK public data systems, MPs say
item.description: Technology committee calls on government to trigger break clause in flashpoint NHS contract

item.title: Premier League clubs could face legal action over sponsorship deals with crypto firms
item.description: UK regulator focuses on teams with partnerships with digital assets companies that are not authorised to operate in Britain

item.title: FirstFT: Tencent moves closer to launching AI agent for WeChat
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China’s hunt for US tungsten, and India Inc gears up for nuclear energy push

item.title: Why ultra-luxury cruises are at risk of running aground
item.description: The core principles of luxury can lose their efficacy on the high seas

item.title: Chinese investors fear missing out on SpaceX IPO after crackdown
item.description: Beijing warns brokers about overseas share sales amid fears of capital flight

item.title: Cliffwater’s flagship private credit fund hit with 17% redemption requests
item.description: Fund with net assets of $31bn aimed at retail investors limits withdrawals in latest sign of exodus from sector

item.title: Could we interest you in $675bn in tech stocks?
item.description: Tech offers more equity to hungry investors
